workflow.name: Analyze alerts from Alertmanager and send diagnostic reports to Slack
workflow.description:
# Overview
This workflow helps automatically analyze alerts occurring in the infrastructure and suggest solutions even before the on-duty engineer sees the alert.
## How It Works
The workflow receives an alert from Alertmanager via Webhook.
The variables required for operation are set.
A prompt is prepared for the agent containing only the data necessary for analysis.
The agent performs diagnostics as described in the system prompt. During operation, it can access various systems via MCP to obtain additional information.
A message in a Slack channel corresponding to the processed alert is found.
A report is sent to the Slack thread.
## How to Use
Generate webhook credentials and use them in Alertmanager.
Add the alert fingerprint to the Slack message template.
Set variables in the SetVars node.
Add your own rules and recommendations to the system prompt.
Run MCP servers.
Choose the Slack channel with alerts.

options.systemMessage:
=You are an senior SRE assistant for a game studio infrastructure. Your main goal is to assist in the investigation of the incident indicated by the Alert.

## Kubernetes Tools
Use these to inspect cluster state: pods, deployments, ingress, services, logs, events, etc
- Read container logs for error investigation
- List resources across namespaces
- Main workload is in namespace: kiss

## Grafana Tools  
Use these to query metrics, logs, dashboards, and alerts:
- **Prometheus**: query_prometheus for PromQL queries (CPU, memory, request rates, error rates). Datasource uid: {{ $('SetVars').first().json.prometheus_uid }}
- **Loki**: query_loki_logs for LogQL log searches. Datasource uid: {{ $('SetVars').first().json.loki_uid }}
- **Dashboards**: search_dashboards, get_dashboard_summary to find relevant dashboards

## Vector store tools
- Read alerts rules to understand the conditions for their activation

## Digitalocean tools:
- Use if you need information about App Platform, Droplets, Kubernetes(DOKS), Networking

## Github tools
- Use this to find out if releases have been executed.
- Search only within the organization playneta.

## Critical Rules for Tool Usage
1. **Never retry a failed tool call more than once.** If a Kubernetes resource (pod, deployment, node) is not found on the first attempt, it likely no longer exists. Do NOT retry the same query.
2. **If any tool returns an error or empty result**, acknowledge it and move on.
3.  Don't make any changes to anything through mcp.


## Common Important rules
- Don't ask anything, analyze it yourself

## Investigation flow recommendations
- Pay attention to the alert labels. They will help you understand what the problem is and where it is.
- Study the alert description and its labels.
- Try executing the request that triggered the alert.

## Output format
### What happened
Description of what happened and what happened to what

### Event timeline
Description of events preceding the alert for more than 10 minutes

### Root cause
No more than two root cause suggestions

### Troubleshooting tips
Step-by-step description of actions for each root cause

When formatting your text, make sure it's easy to read in Slack.

parameters.toolDescription: Use this tool to fetch knowledge from the database.

parameters.content:
# Input chain















Receive and format alerts from Alertmanager

# MCP servers
Add correct URLs for remote MCPs
Use following mcp:
* [grafana-mcp](https://github.com/grafana/mcp-grafana)
* [kubernetes-mcp-server](https://github.com/containers/kubernetes-mcp-server)
* [digitalocean-mcp](https://github.com/digitalocean/digitalocean-mcp)
* [gitlab-mcp](https://github.com/zereight/gitlab-mcp)
Create QdrantAPI credentials

# Output chain
















Post generated report to Slack

# Overview
This workflow helps automatically analyze alerts occurring in the infrastructure and suggest solutions even before the on-duty engineer sees the alert.
## How It Works
The workflow receives an alert from Alertmanager via Webhook.
The variables required for operation are set.
A prompt is prepared for the agent containing only the data necessary for analysis.
The agent performs diagnostics as described in the system prompt. During operation, it can access various systems via MCP to obtain additional information.
A message in a Slack channel corresponding to the processed alert is found.
A report is sent to the Slack thread.
## How to Use
Generate webhook credentials and use them in Alertmanager.
Add the alert fingerprint to the Slack message template.
Set variables in the SetVars node.
Add your own rules and recommendations to the system prompt.
Run MCP servers.
Choose the Slack channel with alerts.
## Requirements:
- OpenAI  or Anthropic API key
- Slack API key
- Google Gemini API key
- Webhook receiver in Alertmanager
- Webhook token
- Qdrant with token
- Gitlab Access key
